run_name: NATP Module 14: Rehabilitative Nursing — Source-First Dry Run
purpose: The purpose of this unit is to introduce the Nurse Assistant to restorative care. Each individual is entitled to reach his/her optimal level of functioning. The Nurse Assistant assists the patient/resident in achieving maximum independent living skills through use of rehabilitative or restorative procedures.

objectives.objective_title: Describe the rehabilitation team, state its purpose, and discuss the role of the Nurse Assistant as a member of the team
objectives.exact_objective_statement: Describe the rehabilitation team, state its purpose, and discuss the role of the Nurse Assistant as a member of the team
objectives.source_excerpt: Describe the rehabilitation team, state its purpose, and discuss the role of the Nurse Assistant as a member of the team. A. Rehabilitation team 1. Rehabilitation is a skilled care provided by licensed therapy staff and their assistants 2. Members a. Patient/resident b. Family members c. Nurse Assistant d. Licensed nursing staff e. Physical Therapist f. Occupational Therapist A. Lecture Module 14- Rehabilitative Nursing g. Speech Therapist B. Discussion h. Physician A. Written test i. Activity Leader B. Demonstrates j. Social Worker k. Clergy consistent application l. Dietitian of Nurse Assistant B. Purpose of rehabilitation responsibilities in 1. Discuss and evaluate patient's/resident's level of functioning promoting self-care 2. Establish patient's/resident's care plan and goals for rehabilitation 3. Evaluate progress and adjust plan of care to achieve patient's/resident's goals with a greater degree of patient/resident independence 4. Restore the patient/resident to his/her optimal level of functioning C. Nurse Assistant responsibilities as a member of the rehabilitation team 1. Participate in patient/resident care planning conferences 2. Observe and report patient's/resident's responses to care 3. Follow the patient's/resident's care plan 4. Encourage the patient/resident to follow the rehabilitation plan 5. Observe and report early signs and symptoms of complications

objectives.objective_title: List the responsibilities of the Nurse Assistant in promoting patient/resident self-care
objectives.exact_objective_statement: List the responsibilities of the Nurse Assistant in promoting patient/resident self-care
objectives.source_excerpt: List the responsibilities of the Nurse Assistant in promoting patient/resident self-care. A. Protect patient's/resident's rights B. Ensure safety and privacy C. Communicate therapeutically � ask patient's/residents' opinions and let them have control D. Adhere to legal and ethical principles E. Follow instructions of immediate supervisor and plan of care Module 14- Rehabilitative Nursing F. Report significant changes in patient/resident condition A. Lecture A. Written test G. Practice appropriate and effective nursing care B. Discussion B. Assist H. Implement rehabilitative measures as ordered, i.e., ROM, training C. Clinical observation patients/residents in plan for patient/resident self-care performing ADLs as I. Encourage patient/resident independence � praise necessary even small accomplishments A. Lecture A. Written test J. Provide emotional support and reassurance B. Discussion B. Uses adaptive/comfort K. Follow the documentation guidelines C. Demonstrate devices L. Concentrate on patient's/resident's abilities D. Catalogs from medical devices appropriately M. Use equipment and devices appropriately (cast, prosthesis, stores to show pictures walker, cane, wedge for post open reduction internal fixation � hip E. Role play students surgery) N. Protect patient/resident from abuse

objectives.objective_title: List common comfort and adaptive devices and explain the purposes of each
objectives.exact_objective_statement: List common comfort and adaptive devices and explain the purposes of each
objectives.source_excerpt: List common comfort and adaptive devices and explain the purposes of each. A. Comfort devices and their purposes: 1. Footboard; prevents plantar flexion 2. Trochanter roll; prevents external rotation and pressure sores 3. Hard splint; prevents contractures 4. Bed cradle; prevents foot drop Module 14- Rehabilitative Nursing 5. Trapeze; strengthens muscles and facilitates movement assisting disabled persons with adaptive devices 6. Sheepskin; avoids friction, skin breakdown F. Return demonstration on range-of-motion manual 7. Heel/elbow protectors; protect against friction, skin breakdown skills and care of casts G. Manual Skills 14.7- 8. Flotation pads, egg crate mattress, water bed, alternating Providing Cast Care pressure mattress; Clinitron bed protects pressure points, prevents skin breakdown 9. Pillows and boosters; provide support, positioning, and prevent contractures 10. Cast/traction; keep the affected site in proper alignment, prevent complication B. Adaptive/self-help devices � their purpose and use: 1. Cuffed or swivel-handled utensils, plate guards or holders eating utensils 2. Long-handled combs/brushes, button hooks, sock puller, specially designed clothing; hygiene and grooming aides 3. Reachers, telephone holder, communication boards; promote independence 4. Artificial limbs, application, care and removal 5. Casts and splints; immobilization, alignment, and support, cast care

objectives.objective_title: Identify steps to prevent complications from inactivity
objectives.exact_objective_statement: Identify steps to prevent complications from inactivity
objectives.source_excerpt: A. Lecture A. Written test Identify steps to prevent complications from inactivity. B. Discussion B. Uses appropriate A. Complications methods to prevent 1. Types: complications from a. Neurological (stress, depression, changes in behavior, inactivity sleep disturbances) b. Respiratory (stasis pneumonia or atelectasis) c. Circulatory 1) Thrombophlebitis 2) Pulmonary embolism d. Musculo-skeletal 1) Contractures � are permanent 2) Osteoporosis � demineralization due to lack of weight bearing 3) Muscle atrophy Module 14- Rehabilitative Nursing e. Integumentary (pressure sores) f. Gastro-intestinal (constipation and decrease of appetite) g. Genito-urinary (kidney stones, urinary tract infection) h. Psychosocial (loneliness, depression) 2. Causes: a. Bed rest b. Prolonged illness c. Immobility due to injury d. Surgery B. Preventive methods 1. Turning/repositioning 2. TCDB (turn, cough, and deep-breathing exercises) 3. Body alignment 4. Range of motion 5. Supportive devices 6. Skin care 7. Encouraging patient/resident independence 8. Toileting 9. Bowel and bladder training 10. Elastic stockings 11. Ambulation is best a. Maintains muscles and bones; moves joints b. Reduces pressure on the skin c. Increases circulation d. Increases respiratory and heart function e. Improves bowel function and promotes emptying of bladder f. Promotes independence and self-esteem g. If patient/resident cannot walk, at least stand, or transfer them to a chair Module 14- Rehabilitative Nursing
objectives.weight_reason: Deterministic source-density allocation from source minimum theory hours; assessment/clinical/certificate time excluded.
content_outline: Source objective statement: Identify steps to prevent complications from inactivity

objectives.source_excerpt: A. Lecture A. Written test Describe range-of-motion exercises (ROM). B. Discussion B. Performs basic range A. Range of motion is movement of joints through their normal range C. Demonstrate ROM; active of motion exercises of movement to the point of resistance or discomfort and passive correctly B. Purpose D. Manual Skills 14.9a - 1. Maintain muscle strength Range of Motion 2. Stimulate circulation exercises 3. Maintain body alignment and make positioning easier E. Manual Skills 14.9b - 4. Prevent thrombophlebitis Performs passive range of 5. Prevent contractures motion - PROM for one C. Frequency knee and one ankle 1. At least three times each day with at least three repetitions of F. Manual skills 14.9c - Passive range of motion- each movement of the joint PROM for one shoulder 2. As indicated in the care plan D. Technique 1. Active ROM a. Best b. Patient/resident moves own joints through their normal range of movement c. Maintains muscle 2. Passive ROM a. Nurse Assistant moves the patient's/resident's joints through their normal range of movement to the point of resistance or discomfort b. Still gets muscle atrophy 3. Active assistive ROM - the Nurse Assistant helps the patient/resident move his/her joints through their normal range of movement to the point of resistance or discomfort or the patient/resident uses a resistive device E. General rules to avoid injury 1. Exercise joint correctly 2. Avoid unnecessary exposure of patient/resident 3. Use good body mechanics Module 14- Rehabilitative Nursing 4. Fully support each extremity A. Lecture A. Written test 5. Move join

objectives.source_excerpt: would help them Identify procedures and devices used to promote mobility and D. Demo, practice ambulation for patients/residents with physical and/or visual E. Review video showing impairment. techniques as needed A. Things to check to determine if patient/resident can be more independent 1. Physical strength and ability 2. Available special training 3. Assistive devices 4. Financial resources 5. Cognitive ability 6. Motivation B. Procedures 1. Transfer techniques � review from Module 5 a. Follow facility policy on lifting and transferring b. Patient/resident weight above 100 lbs. requires two person assists c. Patient/resident with osteoporosis should be transferred using manual team lift 2. Gait training 3. Training in self-transfer techniques 4. Use of gait belt for ambulation 5. Check to see if the patient/resident can sit unassisted and steadily, as well as stand a few seconds alone C. Assistive devices 1. Cane 2. Walker 3. Wheelchair 4. Transfer board A. Lecture Module 14- Rehabilitative Nursing 5. Braces, splints, and prostheses B. Discussion 6. Service animals (Seeing Eye and Hearing Ear dogs) A. Written test 7. Braille B. Practices consistent, 8. Modifications to accommodate wheelchair access 9. Disabled parking effective therapeutic D. objectives.objective_title: Discuss the process of documentation and the Nurse Assistant role in care plan meeting
objectives.exact_objective_statement: Discuss the process of documentation and the Nurse Assistant role in care plan meeting
objectives.source_excerpt: A. Lecture Discuss the process of documentation and the Nurse Assistant B. Discussion role in care plan meeting. A. Nurse Assistant helps the team members in completing the Patient/resident Assessment Instrument (RAI) as mandated by federal government Module 14- Rehabilitative Nursing B. RAI consist of three parts: C. Sample minimum data set review the 1. Minimum Data Set 3.0 (MDS 3.0) � assessment tool 3.0 Form patient/resident 2. Triggers � condition that identifies problem (risk factors) that clinical record under may require care plan D. Download MDS form at MDS section and Care 3. Patient/resident Assessment Protocol (RAPs) � list of http://www.cms.hhs.gov/ Plan section of the information and guidelines that will link to care plan goals record E. Show sample of Care Plan C. Interpret the codes as C. MDS Assessment is utilized and completed upon admission, Form appropriate that reflect quarterly, annually, and when there is a significant change in patient/resident condition functioning D. Nurse Assistant will provide information to complete the appropriate section(s) of the MDS E. Care Plan will be reviewed during IDT meeting F. Team discusses the appropriate interventions; updates the information that reflects current patient/resident functioning G. Nurse Assistant contributes valuable information because of being the direct patient care provided, especially in the areas of activities of daily living Module 14- Rehabilitative Nursing
